title: Nursery Rhyme. CCCCVI. Jingles.
content:
[The first line of the following is the burden of a song in the 'Tempest,' act i, sc. 2. and also of one in the 'Merchant of Venice, act iii, sc. 2.]

        Ding dong bell,
        Pussy's in the well!
        Who put her in? - 
        Little Tommy Lin.
        Who pulled her out? - 
        Dog with long snout.
        What a naughty boy was that
        To drown poor pussy-cat,
        Who never did any harm,
        But kill'd the mice in his father's barn.
meaning:
"Nursery Rhyme. CCCCVI. Jingles." is a quintessential example of Unknown's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
